cow.name: Foot-and-Mouth Disease (FMD)
cow.symptoms: Fever, blisters on mouth/feet, drooling, lameness
cow.treatment: Vaccinate every 6 months. Isolate infected animals immediately. Provide soft food.
cow.prevention: Regular FMD vaccination at 4 months, booster at 9 months, then every 6 months.

cow.name: Mastitis
cow.symptoms: Swollen udder, abnormal/clotted milk, pain during milking, fever
cow.treatment: Consult vet for antibiotics. Hot water fomentation. Complete milking essential.
cow.prevention: Maintain udder hygiene, use teat dip after milking, clean bedding.

cow.name: Bloat
cow.symptoms: Swollen left abdomen, difficulty breathing, restlessness, stops eating
cow.treatment: Use anti-bloat oil. Emergency: use trocar for severe cases. Walk the animal.
cow.prevention: Avoid sudden diet changes. Don't feed wet legumes. Provide dry fodder before green.

cow.name: Brucellosis
cow.symptoms: Abortion, reduced milk yield, retained placenta, swollen joints
cow.treatment: No effective treatment. Cull positive animals. Test regularly (Rose Bengal test).
cow.prevention: Vaccinate female calves at 4-8 months (S19 vaccine). Test before buying new animals.

cow.name: Black Quarter (BQ)
cow.symptoms: Sudden lameness, swelling in muscles, high fever, crepitating sound on pressing
cow.treatment: Requires immediate penicillin treatment. Consult vet urgently.
cow.prevention: Vaccinate annually before monsoon. Keep animals on dry ground.

cow.name: Hemorrhagic Septicemia (HS)
cow.symptoms: High fever, swollen throat/brisket, difficulty breathing, sudden death
cow.treatment: Oxytetracycline or sulfonamides if caught early. Usually fatal if delayed.

cow.name: Theileriosis
cow.symptoms: High fever, swollen lymph nodes, anemia, jaundice, reduced milk
cow.treatment: Buparvaquone injection. Supportive therapy with iron supplements.
cow.prevention: Tick control using acaricides. Vaccinate in endemic areas at 3 months.

buffalo.name: Hemorrhagic Septicemia (HS)
buffalo.symptoms: High fever, swollen throat, rapid breathing, sudden death
buffalo.treatment: Oxytetracycline if caught early. Usually fatal within 24 hours.
buffalo.prevention: Annual vaccination before monsoon. Most critical vaccine for buffalo.

buffalo.name: Mastitis
buffalo.symptoms: Swollen udder, clotted milk, pain, reduced yield
buffalo.treatment: Antibiotics under vet guidance. Dry cow therapy recommended.
buffalo.prevention: Clean milking practices. Teat dipping. Proper dry period management.

buffalo.name: Foot Rot
buffalo.symptoms: Lameness, foul smell from feet, swelling between toes
buffalo.treatment: Clean wound, apply antiseptic. Antibiotics if severe.
buffalo.prevention: Keep floors clean and dry. Copper sulfate foot bath. Regular hoof trimming.

buffalo.name: Theileriosis
buffalo.symptoms: High fever, enlarged lymph nodes, anemia, weakness
buffalo.treatment: Buparvaquone injection. Blood transfusion in severe cases.
buffalo.prevention: Tick control with acaricides. Keep sheds clean.

buffalo.name: Parasitic Infections
buffalo.symptoms: Weight loss, rough coat, diarrhea, pot belly in calves
buffalo.treatment: Ivermectin or Albendazole. Include liver fluke treatment.
buffalo.prevention: Regular deworming every 3 months. Avoid stagnant water grazing.

goat.name: PPR (Peste des Petits Ruminants)
goat.symptoms: Fever, nasal discharge, mouth ulcers, severe diarrhea, pneumonia
goat.treatment: No specific treatment. Supportive care: fluids, antibiotics for secondary infections.
goat.prevention: Vaccinate once at 3 months — provides lifelong immunity.

goat.name: Enterotoxemia
goat.symptoms: Sudden death, bloat, abdominal pain, convulsions, diarrhea
goat.treatment: Antitoxin if caught early. Usually fatal by the time symptoms appear.
goat.prevention: Vaccinate kids at 4 months, booster in 15 days, then every 6 months.

goat.name: Foot Rot
goat.symptoms: Lameness, swelling between toes, foul smell
goat.treatment: Trim hooves, apply antiseptic. Zinc sulfate foot bath.
goat.prevention: Regular hoof trimming. Keep pens dry. Foot bath every 2 weeks.

goat.name: Pneumonia
goat.symptoms: Coughing, nasal discharge, rapid breathing, fever, loss of appetite
goat.prevention: Proper ventilation in sheds. Avoid overcrowding. Vaccinate against PPR.

goat.name: Internal Parasites (Worms)
goat.symptoms: Anemia (pale eyelids), weight loss, diarrhea, bottle jaw
goat.treatment: Deworm every 2-3 months. Use FAMACHA scoring for targeted treatment.
goat.prevention: Rotational grazing. Don't graze on wet pastures. Regular fecal egg counts.

sheep.name: Blue Tongue
sheep.symptoms: Swollen tongue (blue), drooling, fever, swelling around mouth/eyes
sheep.treatment: No direct treatment. Supportive care: soft food, anti-inflammatory drugs.
sheep.prevention: Vaccinate before monsoon in endemic areas. Control midge vectors.

sheep.name: Sheep Pox
sheep.symptoms: Fever, skin nodules, scabs, nasal discharge, loss of appetite
sheep.treatment: No cure. Treat secondary infections. Isolate infected animals.
sheep.prevention: Vaccinate annually. Quarantine new animals for 2 weeks.

sheep.name: Liver Fluke
sheep.symptoms: Weight loss, bottle jaw (submandibular edema), anemia, diarrhea
sheep.treatment: Deworm with Triclabendazole. Supportive care with iron supplements.
sheep.prevention: Avoid wet grazing areas. Deworm strategically before and after monsoon.

sheep.name: Enterotoxemia
sheep.prevention: Vaccinate at 4 months, booster in 15 days, then every 6 months.

sheep.name: Foot Rot
sheep.symptoms: Lameness, foul smell, swelling between toes
sheep.treatment: Hoof trimming. 10% zinc sulfate foot bath. Antibiotics if severe.
sheep.prevention: Regular hoof trimming. Foot bath weekly. Keep housing dry.

poultry.name: Newcastle Disease (Ranikhet)
poultry.symptoms: Respiratory distress, green droppings, twisted neck, paralysis, drop in egg production
poultry.treatment: No treatment. Cull severely affected birds. Supportive care for mild cases.
poultry.prevention: Vaccinate at Day 7, Day 21, and every 3 months (Lasota/R2B).

poultry.name: Fowl Pox
poultry.symptoms: Scabs/warts on comb, wattle, and around eyes. Wet form: lesions in mouth/throat
poultry.treatment: No direct cure. Remove scabs, apply iodine. Antibiotics for secondary infections.

poultry.name: Coccidiosis
poultry.symptoms: Bloody/watery droppings, weakness, ruffled feathers, drop in feed intake
poultry.treatment: Anticoccidial drugs (Amprolium). Rehydration. Vitamin K supplement.
poultry.prevention: Keep litter dry. Anticoccidial in feed. Good ventilation.

poultry.name: Infectious Bursal Disease (IBD/Gumboro)
poultry.symptoms: Feather ruffling, white watery diarrhea, dehydration, high mortality in young birds
poultry.treatment: No specific treatment. Supportive: electrolytes, vitamins.

poultry.name: Avian Influenza (Bird Flu)
poultry.symptoms: Sudden death, swollen head, purple discoloration of comb, drop in egg production
poultry.treatment: No treatment. Cull all birds. Report to authorities immediately.
poultry.prevention: Strict biosecurity. No contact with wild birds. Control visitors.

poultry.name: Marek's Disease
poultry.symptoms: Paralysis of legs/wings, weight loss, grey iris, tumors in organs
poultry.prevention: Vaccinate at hatchery (Day 1). Buy from vaccinated sources only.
